testingFrequency: Commercial annually; residential irrigation on the JEA residential checkup cycle, generally every two years
dueBasis: JEA runs a two-track system: commercial services are tested annually, while residential irrigation and reclaimed-water protections follow the utility checkup program. Commercial failed tests must be repaired or replaced and retested within 30 days.
coveredPropertyTypes: Fire sprinkler services under JEA rules
coveredDeviceTypes: Double check assemblies on certain fire lines
officialListLabel: Open JEA's qualified backflow vendors list
penalties: Commercial failed tests must be repaired or replaced and retested within 30 days. Residential customers who do not submit results within 45 days can have a utility-dispatched tester sent and a $35 fee added to the bill.
sourceExcerpt: JEA is one of the strongest Florida utilities because it openly separates commercial annual testing, residential irrigation rules, and a live qualified-vendor list with compliance scores.
verdictSummary: JEA is a flagship Florida utility because it publishes qualified vendors, annual commercial rules, and a real residential irrigation workflow.
whoIsAffected: JEA commercial customers, residential irrigation customers, reclaimed-water users, and certain fire-line services that require backflow protection or testing.
residentialNotes: Residential demand is strong because JEA publishes a utility-run checkup program and a $35 fallback test fee if the owner does nothing.
This is not a generic Jacksonville plumbing page; it is a real utility workflow.
commercialNotes: Commercial value is very strong because JEA publishes annual testing, 30-day failed-test repair timing, and vendor compliance scores.
This is one of the cleanest monetizable approved-tester routes in the Florida cluster.

annualTesting.summary: JEA requires annual testing for commercial services, while residential irrigation assemblies run on the utility's residential checkup cycle.
highlights: Commercial failed tests must be repaired or replaced and retested within 30 days.
Residential customers who do not submit results within 45 days can trigger a JEA-dispatched tester and a $35 bill charge.
workflowSteps: Confirm whether the service is commercial or residential irrigation.
Choose a qualified vendor from JEA's list or follow the utility-run checkup program.
Complete the test and clear any failed-test repairs inside the utility timeline.

irrigation.summary: JEA is especially strong for irrigation because the residential program explicitly covers irrigation meters, non-meter irrigation, and reclaimed-water irrigation connections.
highlights: Residential irrigation devices are part of a utility-run checkup program.
Reclaimed-water irrigation has its own protective rules.
Qualified vendors are public and easy to route to.
workflowSteps: Confirm whether the system uses an irrigation meter, main water service, or reclaimed water.
Use a qualified JEA vendor or follow the utility-scheduled checkup program.
Repair and retest failed devices inside the JEA timeline.

fireLine.summary: Some fire sprinkler lines may use double checks under specific circumstances and still sit inside the utility's annual commercial workflow.
highlights: Some fire sprinkler lines may use DC assemblies under JEA policy.
The qualified vendor list includes strong fire-protection companies.
workflowSteps: Confirm the fire-line assembly type under JEA rules.
Use a qualified vendor that handles the relevant assembly class.
Retest failed assemblies within 30 days if required.
Identify whether the service is commercial or residential irrigation.
Use a qualified JEA backflow vendor or follow the utility-run residential program.
Complete the test on the correct cycle.
Repair and retest failed assemblies inside the JEA timeline.
failureHighlights: JEA publishes both qualified vendors and compliance scores.
Commercial failed tests have a 30-day repair and retest window.
Residential non-response can trigger a utility-dispatched test and fee.

costBand.testingRange: Testing is market-priced when the owner chooses a vendor, but JEA can bill $35 for a utility-arranged residential test if the owner does not submit results in time.
costBand.repairRetestRange: Repair and retest cost depends on device class, especially for commercial and fire-side assemblies.
costBand.pricingNotes: JEA's strongest local value is a fully operational utility workflow plus a ranked vendor surface.
